title: Apache Tomcat Valve Request Filtering and Access Pattern Audit
dataSources: `index=web` `sourcetype=tomcat:access` (Catalina HTTP access log with RemoteAddrValve / ErrorReportValve outcomes)
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder; Splunk Add-on for JMX (`Splunk_TA_jmx`) for metrics; file inputs for `tomcat:catalina`, `tomcat:access`, `tomcat:manager`
spl:
index=web sourcetype="tomcat:access"
| where status=403 OR status=401
| stats count by cs_uri_stem, remote_host, status
| sort - count
| head 50
description: Valves such as `RemoteAddrValve` and security constraints emit 403/401 in access logs. Auditing stems and sources validates rules and spots probing.
value: Supports zero-trust and WAF-adjacent controls with evidence of enforcement.
implementation: Enable Access Log Valve in `server.xml`; sync field order with `props.conf`. Correlate 403 spikes with `RemoteAddrValve` deny rules.
visualization: Time charts for utilization, tables for top URIs and deploy events, single-value alerts.
grandmaExplanation: We review blocked and challenged requests so security valves behave as intended.
